title: Make util and copier build on FreeBSD
body:
The mknod syscall takes a uint64 value for dev on FreeBSD.

Signed-off-by: Doug Rabson <dfr@rabson.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

-->

> /kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Needed for porting buildah to FreeBSD.

comments.body: @dfr Could you add `[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]` in the commit message.
The compiler's complaining about mismatched "//go:build" and "// +build" directives, in this case because the new files don't include the older style.  Otherwise, LGTM.

Both build failures look like this:
```
[+2568s] ok 684 buildah version current in package # skip buildah binary is not owned by package
```

edit: Actually, I don't think that message is an error. In both, I think all the tests are passing but the exit code is still non-zero.

The "not ok" tests are the ones to look for there.  Restarted the two that have failed because they're both getting TLS-related errors while talking to a registry, which is hopefully just a transient error.
